title: Brain Recovery: Why Sobriety Feels Worse First
description: Long-form YouTube explainer on the early-sobriety brain — dopamine downregulation, 'PAWS' symptoms, when fog lifts and what to expect. Anti-doom-spiral framing: this is biology, not failure.
hook: Your brain isn't broken. Here's what's actually happening.

cta.text: Subscribe for the rest of the recovery science series

keyMessage: The first month of sobriety feels worse because your brain is recalibrating, not because something is wrong with you.
angle: Reframe early-sobriety distress as a feature of recovery, not a bug
